concept.name: column-gap
concept.explain:
Use column-gap to control the space between columns in the grid.

Example:
column-gap: 12px;

This adds horizontal space between columns without changing the car's placement.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in the same place and adjust the horizontal gap to match the parking bay.
hints: Use column-gap to change the space between columns.
Only the horizontal space needs to change.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-width: 132px; justify-self: start;

concept.name: row-gap
concept.explain:
Use row-gap to control the space between rows in the grid.

Example:
row-gap: 14px;

This adds vertical space between rows without changing the car's placement.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in the same place and adjust the vertical gap to match the parking bay.
hints: Use row-gap to change the space between rows.
Only the vertical space needs to change.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-height: 134px; align-self: start;

concept.name: gap
concept.explain:
Use gap as a shorthand for both row-gap and column-gap.

Example:
gap: 16px 10px;

The first value sets the row gap. The second value sets the column gap.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in the same place and adjust both gaps to match the parking bay.
hints: Use gap to control both row and column spacing.
The first value is for rows. The second value is for columns.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-width: 130px; min-height: 136px; justify-self: start; align-self: start;
